expected_gaps.description: A cybersecurity supply chain risk management program, strategy, objectives, policies, and processes are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address supply chain risk management program or strategy
expected_gaps.evidence: No mention of supply chain, vendor security, or third-party risk management in governance sections

expected_gaps.description: Suppliers and third-party partners of information systems, components, and services are identified, prioritized, and assessed using a cyber supply chain risk assessment process
expected_gaps.reason: Policy lacks supplier and third-party cybersecurity assessment processes
expected_gaps.evidence: No supplier assessment or third-party security evaluation mentioned

expected_gaps.description: Contracts with suppliers and third-party partners are used to implement appropriate measures designed to meet the objectives of an organization's cybersecurity supply chain risk management plan and applicable regulatory requirements
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not specify cybersecurity requirements in supplier contracts
expected_gaps.evidence: No contractual security requirements or supplier obligations mentioned

expected_gaps.description: Suppliers and third-party partners are routinely assessed using audits, test results, or other forms of evaluations to confirm they are meeting their contractual obligations
expected_gaps.reason: Policy lacks ongoing supplier security monitoring and assessment
expected_gaps.evidence: No supplier audit or evaluation processes described

expected_gaps.description: Response and recovery planning and testing are conducted with suppliers and third-party providers
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not include suppliers in incident response or recovery planning
expected_gaps.evidence: Incident response section does not mention supplier coordination

expected_gaps.description: The organizational mission, objectives, stakeholders, and activities are understood and prioritized; this information is used to inform cybersecurity roles, responsibilities, and risk management decisions
expected_gaps.reason: Policy lacks explicit connection between organizational mission and cybersecurity strategy
expected_gaps.evidence: No organizational context or mission alignment in cybersecurity framework

expected_gaps.description: Internal and external stakeholders are understood, and their needs and expectations regarding cybersecurity are understood and considered
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not identify or address stakeholder cybersecurity expectations
expected_gaps.evidence: No stakeholder analysis or expectations documented

expected_gaps.description: Legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements regarding cybersecurity — including privacy and civil liberties obligations — are understood and managed
expected_gaps.reason: Policy mentions compliance generically but lacks specific legal/regulatory requirement management
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 9 mentions compliance but does not detail specific regulatory requirements or management processes

notes: The ISMS policy intentionally omits all GV.SC (Supply Chain Risk Management) subcategories
The policy also omits GV.OV (Organizational Context) subcategories
These are critical governance gaps that should be detected by Stage A analysis
At least 7 out of 8 gaps (87.5%) must be detected to pass validation
